---
title: "03 Figures"
author: "MAA, Data Science Lab, Progress and Health Public Foundation (FPS), Regional Ministry of Health, Andalucía, Sevilla"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

Template author: MAA, Data Science Lab, Progress and Health Public Foundation (FPS), Regional Ministry of Health, Andalucía, Sevilla.

## Data Scientist Style Recommendations

Use this notebook only to visualize validated outputs from upstream notebooks. Cohort flow counts must match `01_dataset.qmd`, table-aligned figures must match `02_tables.qmd`, and model-performance figures should come from exported `03_model.qmd` results rather than recomputed logic. Keep figure naming centralized, make labels publication-ready, and export one stable file per figure. All code, comments, labels, and narrative must be written in English.

## Setup

This section initializes the environment for figure generation.

Typical libraries:
- pandas
- numpy
- matplotlib
- seaborn
- pathlib

Guidelines:
- load only visualization-related libraries
- do not perform transformations here
- create `figures/` before exporting any figure
- write every exported figure under `figures/`

## Load Analytical Inputs

This section loads the data required for plotting.

Possible inputs:
- final dataset from 01_dataset
- tables from 02_tables

Guidelines:
- do not recompute analytical results
- use previous outputs as source of truth

## Cohort Flow Figures

This section visualizes cohort selection.

Typical outputs:
- CONSORT diagram
- flow plots

Guidelines:
- counts must match 01_dataset exactly

## Data Completeness Figures

This section visualizes missingness.

Typical outputs:
- missingness bar plots
- heatmaps

Libraries:
- seaborn
- matplotlib
- missingno (optional)

## Baseline Distribution Figures

This section visualizes baseline variables.

Typical outputs:
- histograms
- density plots
- boxplots
- count plots

Guidelines:
- align with Table 1 variables

## Covariate Balance Figures

This section visualizes covariate balance.

Typical outputs:
- Love plots (SMD)
- pre/post weighting plots

Guidelines:
- must match SMD values in 02_tables
- if 95% CI for SMD is available, it may be displayed

## Outcome Figures

This section visualizes outcomes.

Typical outputs:
- event rate bar plots
- Kaplan–Meier curves (if applicable)
- cumulative incidence plots

Guidelines:
- must match definitions from 01_dataset
- complement, not duplicate, tables

## Subgroup Figures

This section visualizes subgroup results.

Typical outputs:
- stratified plots
- forest plots
- faceted distributions

Guidelines:
- consistent subgroup definitions with 02_tables
- avoid small unstable groups

## Sensitivity Analysis Figures

This section visualizes robustness analyses.

Typical outputs:
- comparison plots (main vs sensitivity)
- alternative cohort visualizations

Guidelines:
- directly comparable with main results

## Figure Styling and Annotation

This section standardizes figure appearance.

Tasks:
- define sizes
- harmonize labels
- consistent legends
- consistent formatting

## Export Figures

This section saves figures.

Formats:
- PNG
- PDF
- SVG

Guidelines:
- one file per figure
- consistent naming

## Session Info

Record environment for reproducibility.
